characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The Ghost with the Most. A shady "bio-exorcist", Betelgeuse (pronounced "Beetlejuice") is a ghost that professionally scares the living out of other ghosts' haunts, and loves his work. He's very easy to summon — saying his name three times is all that's needed — but very difficult to get rid of once he's there, because he's steadfastly determined to remain in the world of the living as long as he can manage. Abhorrent Admirer: By the sequel he appears to have genuinely fallen for Lydia, calling her "the love of my life" when they're face-to-face for the first time in decades. Lydia still finds him creepy and disgusting and repeatedly tells him to leave her alone. Abnormal Limb Rotation Range: Betelgeuse's head spins freely, apparently not within his complete control.noteIn the original script, Betelgeuse died by suicide via hanging. Given how ghosts either reflect or maintain attributes from their death, this may be from cracked or outright destroyed neck vertebrae. AcCENT upon the Wrong SylLABle: Adam Maitland continually mispronounces his name different ways: "Beetle-geiss", "Beetle-meier" or even "This beetle guy". Affably Evil: Zig-zags between this andFaux Affably Evil. While Betelgeuse is not a friend to anyone and is unsafe for women, he's just so damncharming. He offers to make Barbara and Adam an Italian dinner, attempts their requested task (though not to their liking), and feels genuinely offended when they interfere. He is initially baffled by Lydia's desire to be dead but offers to help since she insists. Despite his quirky traits, Betelgeuse is ultimately a crude, perverted troublemaker without restraint or self-control, making him dangerous to be around for long. His enjoyment may stem from a childish desire to torment and nearly kill with his pranks. He is shown to be reckless and harmful, evidenced by his nearly killing Lydia's father as a snake, attacking Maxie Dean and his spouse, and using Lydia to gain permanent access to the living world.

characters.name: Betelgeuse

characters.name: Adam Maitland
characters.description: A humble countryman who runs a hardware store and enjoys building miniature models. AcCENT upon the Wrong SylLABle: When Adam finds the advert for Betelgeuse, he actually says the name three times, but because he pronounces it "Bay-til Guy-ce", the incantation doesn't work. Body Horror: Having his head cut off, then stretching his nose out while shoving his hand into the back of his head and growing eyes on his fingers. Establishing Character Moment: Adam and the spider. Instead of squashing it, he only comments on its size and lets it go free, showing him to be anice guy. Fanboy: He's shown to be one ofHarry Belafonte, as he enjoys listening to his music while working on the model. As a ghost, he tries to frighten everyone (save for Lydia) away by possessing everyone into doing a dance number to "Day-O (The Banana Boat Song),” and at the end of the film, he rewards Lydia for getting an A on her math test by using his powers to play "Jump in the Line" while Lydia dances in the air.

characters.name: Barbara Maitland
characters.description: A homemaker who was planning on starting a family before her death. Action Girl: A downplayed example, since she doesn't have too many action scenes, but the scenes where she deals with the sandworm definitely put her here. Big Damn Heroes:Just as Betelgeuse is about to marry Lydia, Barbara comes in riding on a Sandworm that eats Betelgeuse, stopping the wedding in the nick of time. Body Horror: Ripping her face off, then stretching out her jaws and setting her eyes in her open mouth. Did You Just Punch Out Cthulhu?: Barbara wangs the Sand Worm right on the nose, causing it to retreat... and then later befriends it!

characters.name: Lydia Deetz
characters.description: A goth girl who can see ghosts. Lydia has a strained relationship with her father and stepmother, neither of whom she feels pays any degree of attention to her, and latches onto the Maitlands as her first genuine friends. In the sequel, some thirty-odd years after the first movie, she has become a medium and paranormal investigator with a fairly successful TV show, alongside a failing relationship with her own (very skeptical) daughter. Abhorrent Admirer: Gains one in the form of Betelgeuse, who, while at first was only interested in marrying her to gain access to the living world, seems to have genuinely fallen for her in the sequel. However, because he has traumatized her as a teenager with his antics and retains hisDirty Old Manstatus, Lydia is as repulsed by him now as she was back then, if not more so. The only reason she agrees to any interaction with him now is only because it's the only option she has. Amazingly Embarrassing Parents: Played for drama. Her show is a source of embarrassment for Astrid, who faces mockery from her fellow students. Amicable Exes: In the sequel, Lydia tells Astrid that her relationship with her husband and Astrid's father, Richard, was over long before his death.But when they meet again in the Afterlife, there's nothing but affection between Lydia and Richard, and they're both very proud of Astrid. Broken Bird: The sequel shows that life and loss through the following three decades have reduced her from aPerky Gothinto this. She eventually regains much of her old spirit, and ironically it's mostly due to dealing with Betelgeuse all over again.Now... if only she could be certain that Betelgeuse is really forever out of her life... again.

characters.name: Delia Deetz
characters.description: An eccentric contemporary artist and Lydia's stepmother. Actually Pretty Funny: Admits she finds the tumultuous mother-daughter relationship between Astrid and Lydia humorous because she thinks it’s karma for how Lydia treated Delia when she was a teenager. Alliterative Name:DeliaDeetz. Arbitrary Skepticism: Subverted. She at first refuses to believe Lydia about her claims about the house being haunted, but comes to believe it immediately after the dinner scene. Her agent Bernard, on the other hand, refuses to believe it happened, even though he was there. It is also an avenue to attract Maxie Dean, an art patron she desperately wants to ingratiate since he and his wife are huge believers in the paranormal. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other:After dying in the sequel, she promises to find Charles and haunt Lydia and Astrid till they live long and happy lives as a family, with Lydia and Astrid looking happy at that.It's also that the one room that Charles asked Delia not to alter in any way remained the same in the sequel, revealing she did honor his request even after Charles died.

characters.name: Charles Deetz
characters.description: A city native that just wants to retreat to the country. And I Must Scream: After being killed in a shark attack, Charles is damned to wander the afterlife with most of his upper body missing. He can still clearly see, hear, and speak though, so how much this actually affects him is unclear. Body Horror: Due to the way the afterlife works,he ends up manifesting not just headless, but also havinga large curved arc of his torso missing.To put the cherry on top, any time he speaks its through what remains of his esophagus, which sputters blood at whoever he's talking to. Bumbling Dad: He's not the most hands-on parent, but he's shown calling Lydia "pumpkin" and demonstrating genuine affection toward her. Everyone Has Standards: He's excited about profiting off his new haunted house, and resorts to a séance/exorcism to force Adam and Barbara to go along with it. However he's unaware that doing so will cause the Maitlands to suffer aFate Worse than Death, and tries unsuccessfully to get Otho to stop the ritual when it becomes apparent that it is harming them.

characters.name: Astrid Deetz
characters.description: Lydia's daughter. Astrid is a firm skeptic with little interest in the supernatural, and is on very poor terms with her mother, whom she views as a fraud and a profound source of social embarassment. All of the Other Reindeer: Her introductory scene shows her being mocked by aGirl Possefor her mom’s TV show. Babies Ever After: Subverted as the endinghas her giving birth to Betelgeuse's inner child, then Lydia wakes up from the nightmare. Brainy Brunette: Her hair is as black as Lydia's, andafter only a quick glance at the Handbook for the Recently Deceased, she's able to find a loophole to invalidate her mother's deal with Betelgeuse, saving Lydia once again from being forced to marry him. Daddy's Girl: She was much closer to her late father, Richard, than she is to her mother. Lydia (who can see other ghosts just fine) being unable to see Astrid's father's spirit only drives them further apart.

characters.name: Juno
characters.description: An undead caseworker and Betelgeuse's old boss. Because You Were Nice to Me: It's implied that part of the reason she makes time for the Maitlands is because they are genuinely nice people who have caught on fairly quick to their situation. Most people the audience sees are cynical or in denial in the afterlife, so its easy to see why she'd care a little more about two people who're still a little out of their depth. Beleaguered Bureaucrat: Juno has a lot of clients, and she's easily frustrated at having to deal with their demands. Big Good: More or less, since she's the highest authority the Maitlands can turn to and is fairly understanding of (if a little put out by) their predicament. She was alsoBetelgeuse'sboss before he did freelance work, and considering how wild and powerful his abilities are, that could not have been a cakewalk, which she explains as clearly as she can to the Maitlands. Cool Old Lady: She's savvy and competent, and easily the most helpful person in the realm of the dead. Naturally, she has a huge waiting list.

characters.name: Miss Argentina
characters.description: A former beauty queen who took her own life, and a receptionist in the Neitherworld. Beleaguered Bureaucrat: Because she took her own life, she must work as a receptionist in the Neitherworld, which regularly gets millions of cases. She admits to the Maitlands that if she had known the fate that awaited her, she wouldn't have had her "little accident." Deadpan Snarker: Likely due to her circumstances, she has this attitude, especially with the Maitlands, who are still figuring out the ways of the Neitherworld. Driven to Suicide: She took her own life by slitting her wrists, which she refers to as her "little accident." Freeze-Frame Bonus: When she brings the Maitlands to Juno's office, her sash reveals she won her title in 1939.

characters.name: Delores
characters.description: Beetlejuice's ex-wife who has unfinished business with him. Ascended Extra: Her severed finger appears in the first movie when Beetlejuice pulls it out to retrieve a wedding ring. He claims she meant nothing to him. But in the sequel, you can understand what he really meant by that. Asshole Victim:After eating the souls of three genuinely innocent bystanders in the realm of the dead, everyone is against her; even Wolf Jackson, in spite of his beef with Beetlejuice and later attempting to arrest him, sympathetically warns him to be on the lookout for her. When she actually encounters the rest of the cast during the climax, the Deetzes actually help Betelgeuse get rid of her by blindsiding her with a hungry Sandworm. Big Bad: Though she is not always onscreen and doesn't meet much of the heroes, Delores' hunt for Beetlejuice drives the plot of the sequel forward and she's the greatest threat to everyone's lives, looming over the desperate actions of Beetlejuice to escape her once and for all at all moments. Big Bad Wannabe: She, along with Jeremy, is set up as the true main bad guy of the movie, whose actions force the heroes and Beetlejuice to form a temporary alliance. However, while she lasts longer than Jeremy, both are ultimately disposed of before the true climax, when Beetlejuice reclaims his position as trueBig Badand final obstacle for Lydia and Astrid.

characters.name: Wolf Jackson
characters.description: A former actor turned ghost detective tracking down Beetlejuice and Delores. Actor Allusion: Willem Dafoe originally played a detective inAmerican Psycho, a film that also included Justin Theroux andan actorfamously known for playing Batman. Body Horror: The side of his head is missing a large amount of flesh, exposing his skull and brain which have since discolored. Catchphrase: Wolf has an obsession with "keepin' it real", which extends to having his secretary keep him from forgetting he wasn't actually a cop when he was alive. Closest Thing We Got: In life, he was an actor famous for playing a hotshot police detective. In the Netherworld, he's been placed in charge of the police department because he's effectively the only ghost they have on hand that's remotely qualified for the job. He keeps having to be reminded that he is not technically a real cop.

characters.name: Bob
characters.description: A shrunken head ghost who works for Betelgeuse, and his second-in-command. Actually a Doombot:Played for Laughs. When Betelgeuse orders Bob to take charge, Bob dresses up like him. When Betelgeuse sees this, he considers Bob to be a good decoy to keep Delores distracted. Character Celebrity Endorsement:He has his own Google commercial. Deader than Dead:He gets his soul consumed by Delores, resulting in him completely ceasing to exist even in the Afterlife. Eye Scream:After he’s turned into an empty, rubbery husk, both of his eyeballs pop like balloons.

characters.name: Otho
characters.description: Delia's friend. An interior designer and amateur occultist. Ambiguously Gay: He's a bachelor obsessed with fashion and interior design and overall behaves in a very effeminate manner. It helped that Shadix himself was gay, and that Otho makes aThe Wizard of Ozreference (the specific allusion being when he quips about a woman still being upset that someone dropped a house on her sister as a nod to the demise of the Wicked Witch of the East, and this being possible evidence of his sexual orientation due toThe Wizard of Ozbeing very popular with the LGBTQ+ community). Everyone Has Standards: Otho is portrayed as a pompous and hubristic thief at worst but he actually seems to be a pretty good friend to Delia and, to a lesser extent, Charles, and is always polite to Lydia and even he's horrified at what his bumbling nearly does to the Maitlands. Hidden Depths: He does seem to have some genuine knowledge of the supernatural and the occult, which may account for why he was able to see the Maitlands partially the moment he arrived in the house, as well as sense Adam running past him when rushing to close the attic door. Impossibly Tacky Clothes: Betelgeuse inflicts Otho with this in the climax.

characters.name: Maxie and Sarah Dean
characters.description: A wealthy real estate couple and the heads of Botco Industries, whom the Deetzes turn to. Actually Pretty Funny: The annoyinglystoicMaxie cannot help himself but giggle whenLydiapoints out that Otho can't even change a tire. Asshole Victim: Both of them are possibly killed by Betelgeuse, but it's hard to feel sorry for them after they spend the entire dinner acting like aloof, callous jerks. The Bore: The movie implies both of them are dead inside. The only time they display any actual emotion is when Lydia unleashes Betelgeuse and he begins to wreak havoc. Even Evil Has Standards: Averted. They are present at the seance and are the only ones who don't seem to care the Maitlands are outright rotting in front of them, even after Lydia protests that they're clearly in pain.

characters.name: Jane & "Little Jane" Butterfield
characters.description: Both realtors, and Barbara's relatives. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Jane may talk sweet, but she only cares about trying to sell the Maitlands' house without their consent. Establishing Character Moment: Jane is introduced driving up to the Maitlands's house despite knowing that they are trying to have a staycation and wish to be left alone, so she can try to convince them to hold a showing for their house to a prospective buyer despite the fact that she isn't the Maitlands's realtor, the house isn't on the market, and they have apparently told her that they have no interest in selling the houserepeatedly. Innocently Insensitive: When trying to talk Barbara into selling their house, Jane offhandedly mentions that the house is meant for a family. Barbara, who's implied to want a child but seems to be infertile, is visibly hurt by the statement. It may not be so innocent as she doesn't even hesitate to sell their house right after they died, and the extended ending confirms she's trying to do the same thing with the Deetzes. Jerkass: Selling her cousin's house after she and her husband just died cements Jane as this. She also let the new owners have all of the Maitlands' stuff, including their photos and wedding clothing. She may not have had any way of knowing about the whole afterlife situation, but it's still flagrantly disrespectful.

characters.name: Rory
characters.description: Lydia's latest fiancé and the producer of her showGhost House. Asshole Victim:Gets eaten by a Sand Worm alongside Delores, normally a horrific fate none would wish on anyone, but considering the reveal of his nature as a despicableGold Diggerand his sleazy attitude, no one sheds a tear for him, especially Lydia and Astrid. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Acts like a charming, down-to-Earth,Understanding Boyfriendto Lydia, but it soon becomes blatantly obvious to the audience (as well as Astrid and Delia) that he's a selfish and manipulative man who doesn't actually care about Lydia's problems.In truth, he's nothing but a sleazy gold-digger who is happy to take advantage of Lydia and is only planning to marry her for the money he'd make as her husband. Crocodile Tears:When he proposes to Lydia and she turns him down, he fakes crying and acts heartbroken to get her to change her mind. Devil in Plain Sight: It's pretty obvious early on that he's a smarmyJerkasswho's only interested in getting into Lydia's good graces while not actually caring about her problems.Him admitting to being aGold Diggerwhile under the influence ofTruth Serumonly reveals that his feelings for Lydia were not even skin-deep.
